meta.setting: Clear Creek, French Gulch, Shasta County, and Wintu homeland
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with land pressure, family survival, road politics, work, hunting, and hard choices
meta.quality_bar: hand-cut, grounded, respectful, table-useful, western-only language

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Wintu homeland around Clear Creek, long presence, villages, seasonal movement, acorns, salmon, burning, Gold Rush violence, displacement, allotments, and perseverance.

research_grounding.title: Redding Rancheria
research_grounding.use: Living Pit River, Wintu, and Yana people; sovereignty, culture, families, and traditional homelands.

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek gold rush context and the arrival of miners, immigrants, roads, and settlement pressure.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Clear Creek Wintu Families cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented respectful field-note or road-camp observation generated later; do not use fake Wintu language
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Clear Creek Kin and Stewards
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing families, workers, elders, interpreters, and land keepers whose home predates every claim paper
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four grounded signs, strengths, or social facts
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, leverage, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Write with care: living families, seasonal knowledge, fishing, acorns, careful fire stewardship, wage work, trade, kin obligations, refusal, and survival under pressure.
Acknowledge Gold Rush violence, disease, theft, forced labor, dispossession, and allotment pressure without making trauma the only identity on the page.
Tie future moves to diplomacy, land memory, food knowledge, warning, community protection, negotiation, route permission, and the right to say no.
Keep the page grounded in 1899 social and environmental reality; do not invent sacred rites, fake language, costume spectacle, or mystical abilities.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: Treat this page like a respectful field note written with the pencil set down whenever someone else should be speaking. Their knowledge is not flavor; it is authority the county keeps trying to misfile.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A Wintu family scene should ask who is standing on whose land, who needs help, who has the right to refuse, and what the road has already taken.
must_preserve: Use respectful 1899 western and regional language only.
Do not invent sacred practices, fake words, or mystical traits.
Represent Native people as living families, workers, stewards, negotiators, and political actors.
The generated page must include image path cast/images/clear-creek-wintu-families.png.
No finished page prose, final moves, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred-invented, or fake-tribal framing
do not reduce Native people to victims, guides, costumes, or symbols
do not use modern slang, modern gear, vehicles, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is respectful, grounded, and useful for play.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to 1899 Jefferson State western play and respects living Native communities.
